evidence.observed: Shipped config set tool_error_rate=0.18 while retry was disabled; wrapper logs AGENT_CALL status and attempts.

findings.root_cause: Intermittent tool failures were enabled and retry was disabled.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_error_rate=0.0, enable retry with backoff, and log non-ok statuses in wrapper.py.

evidence.observed: Shipped config used temperature=1.6 and the original prompt did not define subtotal, discount, shipping, or integer division.

findings.root_cause: High sampling temperature and vague arithmetic instructions made totals and coupon application inconsistent.
findings.suggested_fix: Lower temperature, enable verify/self_consistency, and require exact formula: subtotal=unit_price*qty; discounted=subtotal*(100-pct)//100; total=discounted+shipping.

evidence.observed: Shipped config disabled normalize_unicode and set catalog_override.macbook.in_stock=false, contradicting normal catalog behavior.

findings.root_cause: Tool inputs and catalog results could be corrupted by disabled Unicode normalization and a bad MacBook override.

evidence.observed: Original prompt only said to help and give a total, with no rule to refuse out-of-stock, unknown, invalid coupon, or unserved shipping cases.

findings.root_cause: The agent could invent a total instead of grounding on check_stock, get_discount, and calc_shipping results.
findings.suggested_fix: Require tool grounding and clear no-total refusals when stock, coupon, or shipping data is invalid.

evidence.observed: Shipped config had tool_budget=0; wrapper telemetry records tools_used/tool_count, with many valid orders requiring only one pass through check_stock/get_discount/calc_shipping.

findings.root_cause: The original prompt/config did not cap repeated or unnecessary tool calls.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_budget=4 and instruct the model to call each needed tool at most once.

evidence.observed: Shipped config had redact_pii=false and the original prompt did not forbid echoing email/phone/card data.

findings.root_cause: Customer PII could be repeated in answers or written to telemetry.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable redact_pii, add a no-PII prompt rule, and redact wrapper outputs/log payloads.

evidence.observed: Wrapper telemetry shows many public calls around 19k total tokens; shipped config used premium tier, verbose_system=true, context_size=8, and max_completion_tokens=2000.

findings.root_cause: Verbose context and uncapped generations inflated token usage and cost.
findings.suggested_fix: Use standard tier, verbose_system=false, smaller context, concise prompt, and lower max_completion_tokens.

evidence.observed: Original verbose_system=true, high token budget, no cache, and no tool budget can create long-tail latency; wrapper telemetry measures reported_latency_ms and wall_ms.

findings.root_cause: Large prompts/context and unnecessary tool calls caused slow tail requests.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable cache, shrink context/system verbosity, cap tools, and keep answers short.

evidence.observed: Shipped config had session_drift_rate=0.06 and context_reset_every=0, so later turns in a session can degrade.

findings.root_cause: Session memory drift accumulated across turns without reset.
findings.suggested_fix: Set session_drift_rate=0.0 and reset context periodically.

evidence.observed: Shipped config had loop_guard=false and max_steps=12; repeated actions can hit max_steps.

findings.root_cause: No loop guard allowed repeated identical tool actions.

evidence.observed: The rules and prompt optimization docs state the private phase adds order-note injection; the original prompt did not say to treat GHI CHU/order notes as data only.

findings.root_cause: Customer-controlled notes can contain fake system instructions, fake prices, or fake discounts that the model may obey.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt says notes are untrusted data, prices/discounts come only from tools, and wrapper sanitizes common instruction-like note text before calling the agent.
